Following the nuptials, a grand reception is planned for May 1 in Hyderabad. This marks a significant personal milestone for the actor, who has upcoming film projects.

articles.title: Song lyric of the day by Taylor Swift: ‘I cry a lot, but I am so productive; it is an art. You know you are good when you can even do it with a broken heart.’
articles.description: Taylor Swift's latest lyric from 'The Tortured Poets Department' reveals a profound message about productivity amidst emotional turmoil. The song suggests that even with a broken heart, one can achieve great things, highlighting the hidden strength and efficiency that can emerge from personal suffering. This resonates with real-world experiences where individuals maintain competence despite internal struggles.

articles.title: Baldoni’s lawyers say 'unlikable' Blake caused business losses
articles.description: Justin Baldoni's legal team countered Blake Lively's claims of financial and reputational damage, attributing her brand's struggles to its own shortcomings, not a smear campaign. They questioned her multi-million dollar loss projections, citing her past earnings and past public missteps. Lively's team insists she suffered significant harm and awaits her chance to testify.

articles.title: The secret molecule behind your sunburn: Why DNA isn’t the real culprit
articles.description: Scientists have discovered that RNA, not DNA, is the primary cause of the burning sensation from sunburn. UV exposure damages RNA, triggering a cellular alarm system that leads to inflammation. This groundbreaking finding shifts our understanding of skin protection, emphasizing the need to shield RNA from sun damage to prevent redness and discomfort.

articles.title: In 1868, a local hunter wandered through the hills and accidentally discovered a prehistoric cave hidden for thousands of years
articles.description: A hunter's chance discovery in 1868 led to the unearthing of the Altamira Cave, revealing prehistoric art. Initially doubted, these 14,000-year-old cave paintings, showcasing remarkable skill, are now recognized as masterpieces. Preserved by a natural rockfall, the cave offers a glimpse into humanity's ancient artistic expression, reminding us that history often lies hidden in plain sight.

articles.title: Sipping hot tea can increase cancer risk? Doctor shares a surprising link and suggests a simple fix
articles.description: Drinking tea very hot, especially above 65°C, has been linked to an increased risk of oesophageal cancer. Studies show that consuming beverages at this temperature can damage the oesophagus lining, leading to chronic inflammation and cellular mutations. Allowing tea to cool for a few minutes before drinking significantly reduces this risk.
